title: Information communications technician
change: Occupational standard and assessment plan revised
overviewOfRole: The core purpose is to deliver effective installation, configuration, monitoring, and support of IT systems and telecommunications services.

knowledges.detail: Approaches to back up and storage solutions for example disaster recovery.
Basic elements of technical documentation and its interpretation for example user instructions, specifications, knowledge bases, manufactory manuals.
Principles of root cause problem solving using fault diagnostics for troubleshooting for example to detect, locate or identify.
Principles of basic network addressing for example how devices communicate on a network using key protocols and associated techniques such as subnet masking.
Basic principles of cloud and cloud-based services for example access and storage.
Fundamental principles of virtual networks and components.
Principles of Equality, Diversity and Inclusion.
Methods of communication including level of technical terminology to use to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Different types of maintenance for example corrective and routine, and preventative measures to reduce the incidence of faults.
Key principles of security for example Confidentiality integrity and Availability CIA.
Fundamentals of physical networks and components including wired and or wireless networks.
Approaches to documenting tasks, findings, actions taken and outcome for example, use of task tracking and ticketing systems and testing.
Methods to keep up to date with emerging and or evolving technologies, their use and or impact on the role.

Approaches to environmental sustainability within the IT sector, for example legislation or standards in the workplace and the impact on their organisation e.g. energy-efficient computing, e waste, reuse and hardware lifecycles.
The role of operating systems within the organisation and the various platforms available depending on business requirements.
Purpose of hardware within the organisation and the various components available depending on business requirements.
Principles of virtualisation, for example hardware, networks or software.
Fundamentals of purpose, creation and maintenance of asset registers.
Fundamentals of planning and implementation of system upgrades and updates and their significance.
Interpretation of log files, event viewer or system tools and their purpose.
Principles of Open systems interconnection Model OSI layers or Transmission Control Protocol and or Internet Protocol TCP  IP stack.
Uses of cloud networking within an organisation for example principles of cloud including cloud platforms, security, storage and firewalls.
The role of networking protocols for example Domain Name System, Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol, File Transfer Protocol, Hypertext Transfer Protocol.
Principles of different area networks for example Local Area Networks and Wide Area Networks.
Basic awareness of the purpose of firewalls.
Different types of connectivity and cabling.
The purpose of digital communications technologies for example, hardware, virtual, cellular technologies or Voice Over Internet Protocol VOIP.
Main factors affecting network performance including faults and error control.
Principles of digital communication test and diagnostic equipment usage.
Basic principles of Virtual Private Network VPN and Remote Access Security for example transmission technologies.

behaviours.detail: Take responsibility for the health and safety of themselves and of others who may be affected by their acts or omissions at work.
Committed to continued professional development to maintain and enhance competence.
Takes responsibility for acting sustainably in their role for example switching off lights and systems when not in use, reducing file size and attachments on emails, and recycling.
Team-focus to support inclusivity. For example, support others, show respect to others, and create and maintain productive working relationships.

skills.detail: Interpret and manage internal or external customer requirements, prioritising in line with organisation's policy.
Select and apply the tools and techniques to resolve basic fault finding.
Apply Continuous Professional Development to support necessary business output and technical developments.
Operate securely across platforms, maintaining the security of data of internal and external stakeholders.
Communicate with stakeholders, keeping them informed of progress and managing escalation where applicable.
Complete documentation relevant to the task for example tickets, dockets, database recording.
Maintain a safe working environment for own personal safety and others in line with organisation's Health and Safety requirement and or policies.
Select and apply tools to test and evaluate basic functionality of system performance.
Carry out maintenance across systems, ensuring organisational compliance at all times.
Install, configure or deploy hardware, software or systems.
Use basic automation scripts to execute tasks for example PowerShell, Linux, Command Line Interface.
Use Cabling or Connectors on equipment in line with technical requirements for example physically or remotely.
Install, configure or deploy network hardware or applications.
Set up storage and data access.
Establish digital communication or telecommunications systems through, for example cabling and connecting equipment
Select and apply tools and or diagnostic equipment, for example, Hardware or Software components, to resolve Communications or Telecommunications requirements.
Apply basic telecommunications activities, in response to an allocated task, designated responsibilities, instructions or customer’s requirements.
Use information to identify operational issues.

englishAndMathsQualifications: <p>English and maths qualifications must be completed in line with the <a rel="noopener" href="https://www.gov.uk/guidance/apprenticeship-funding-rules" target="_blank">apprenticeship funding rules</a>.</p>
reviewDetails: this apprenticeship will be reviewed in accordance with our change request policy.
clusterDescription: Help internal and external customers to install and use IT technology along with trouble - shooting their problems. Skills are developed across IT security and data management

duties.dutyDetail: Provide technical support to customers both internal and external through a range of communication channels
Establish and diagnose ICT problems and or faults using the required troubleshooting methodology and tools
Interpret technical specifications for the ICT task
Apply security policies to ICT tasks in line with organisational requirements
Undertake processes with tools and technologies to resolve ICT technical issues
Communicate with all levels of stakeholders, talking them through steps to take to resolve issues or set up systems, keeping them informed of progress and managing escalation and expectations
Apply testing methodologies to hardware or software or cabling assets
Practice guided continuous self-learning to keep up to date with technological developments to enhance skills and take responsibility for own professional development
Document or escalate ICT tasks to ensure a clear audit trail and progression of issues
Address IT issues by prioritising in response to customer service level agreements
Administer security access requirements and permissions for stakeholders escalating as necessary for example password resets
Install and configure software and hardware for example: components, mobile apps, printers, projectors, scanners and cameras.
Support the roll out of upgrades or new systems or applications
Complete cabling tasks for example coaxial, copper or fibre.
Deliver network tasks prioritising security with a view to mitigating and defending against security risks.
Install and configure software and physical or virtual network hardware for example: network devices, switches or routers
Install and commission computer or digital communications hardware
Maintain computer systems or digital communications technologies
Research solutions to maintain network communication architectures
Monitor and report digital communication  or communications systems performance to enable service delivery.
